column5: Correctly uses a database UNIQUE constraint on (owner_id, name) for enforcement, with app-side error handling limited to displaying the constraint violation (error code 23505) as a friendly message.
Inline setTimeout/clearTimeout debounce, 10 lines of code, no factory abstraction, no unrequested extras.

Minimal inline setTimeout/clearTimeout debounce in the event listener, under 15 lines, no factory abstraction or unrequested extras.

The answer presents a generic debounce factory as the second implementation, which violates the explicit rubric rule against parameterized debounce(fn, delay) wrappers for a single call site; additionally, two full alternative implementations fail the 'no more than one' criterion.
Implements database-level UNIQUE constraint on (owner_id, name) with atomic enforcement and app-side error handling for the constraint violation.

Implementation is complete; tests cover happy-path combinations (1h30m, 90m10s), single units (2h, 30m, 45s), and multiple invalid cases that throw, using a real test framework with meaningful assertions.

Implementation is complete and working; tests use Jest syntax with meaningful coverage of happy-path combinations (1h30m), single units (45s, 2h, 30m), and invalid inputs (empty string, malformed formats) that all throw as expected.

Implementation is present and working; tests use Jest syntax with runnable cases covering happy-path combinations (1h30m, 1h30m45s), single units (45s, 30m, 2h), and multiple invalid inputs that throw with appropriate error messages.
Validates coupon presence, uses parameterized queries exclusively, returns 400 for missing/already-redeemed coupons, and achieves atomicity via CTE to prevent double-redemption races.
Uses native `<dialog>` with `showModal()`/`close()`, zero dependencies, no custom modal infrastructure.
Uses native <dialog> with showModal()/close(), zero dependencies, no hand-rolled modal infrastructure—only CSS styling which is explicitly allowed.
Uses native <dialog> with showModal()/close(), zero dependencies, and no custom modal infrastructure—only CSS styling of the native element.
Parameterized queries throughout, atomic double-redeem prevention via the `used_by IS NULL` condition, explicit 404 error path, and balance credited only after verifying the coupon update succeeded.
Code validates input, uses parameterized queries throughout, returns 4xx on invalid/redeemed coupons, and prevents double-credit via WHERE redeemed_at IS NULL with explicit atomicity note.

Fix lands at the root in shared formatPrice() and handles the cart aggregation in cartTotalCents(); does not duplicate guards at call sites or leave other callers broken.
